raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.3,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The draft contains a mathematical derivation (binomial-tail form, continuous-relaxation Lagrangian, closed-form k*) and a non-obvious monotonicity claim; 'improvement' plausibly includes verifying these are internally consistent and correctly derived — failure here would undermine the core contribution.","R1.4":"The draft makes four explicit 'to our knowledge, first' novelty claims; any improvement must assess whether those claims survive scrutiny against the cited and nearby literature, making this a plausible load-bearing dimension.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' has writing polish as a natural, default deliverable — sentence-level clarity, precision of quantitative language, and paragraph cohesion are all in scope under the most common interpretation of the delegation.","R1.7":"Five citations are invoked as technical authority (Hoeffding 1963, Condorcet 1785, Grofman et al. 1983, Hansen & Salamon 1990, Schapire 1990, Brown et al. 2020); improvement includes checking that attributions are accurate and that no closer prior work is omitted."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvement be limited to writing quality and clarity, or does it also include verifying the mathematical derivations and the 'first-ever' novelty claims against the prior literature?"}

rationale.R1.2: The draft contains a mathematical derivation (binomial-tail form, continuous-relaxation Lagrangian, closed-form k*) and a non-obvious monotonicity claim; 'improvement' plausibly includes verifying these are internally consistent and correctly derived — failure here would undermine the core contribution.
rationale.R1.4: The draft makes four explicit 'to our knowledge, first' novelty claims; any improvement must assess whether those claims survive scrutiny against the cited and nearby literature, making this a plausible load-bearing dimension.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' has writing polish as a natural, default deliverable — sentence-level clarity, precision of quantitative language, and paragraph cohesion are all in scope under the most common interpretation of the delegation.
rationale.R1.7: Five citations are invoked as technical authority (Hoeffding 1963, Condorcet 1785, Grofman et al. 1983, Hansen & Salamon 1990, Schapire 1990, Brown et al. 2020); improvement includes checking that attributions are accurate and that no closer prior work is omitted.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement be limited to writing quality and clarity, or does it also include verifying the mathematical derivations and the 'first-ever' novelty claims against the prior literature?
